title: The entrepreneur connecting tourists to African hospitality
content:
Nigerian entrepreneur Rory Okoli started her career in Nigeria's hospitality industry.

That experience, along with travelling across Africa, showed her it was not always easy for tourists to organise African trips.

So, she launched TripZapp with the aim of making it simple for travellers to find, book and pay for African travel.

In particular she wanted to connect locally-owned businesses with tourists.

That involved integrating the latest payment and productivity tools into the firm's operations.

Now TripZapp offers all sorts of adventures, from swimming with turtles in Zanzibar, to hot air balloon rides in Egypt.

This is the fifth part in series on technology in Africa.

A commercial flight from Dubai arrived in Dublin on Wednesday night - the first in a number of days after the United States-Israeli attacks on Iran.
